title: Dreamy Irish Potato Bao
description: A sweet and pillowy Irish dish featuring sweet chilli sauce and ferment-jang, perfect for a hangover cure, and jain-friendly.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine grated potatoes, sweet chilli sauce, and ferment-jang, and mix well.
In a separate bowl, whisk together flour, yeast, salt, and sugar.
Add the dry ingredients to the potato mixture and stir until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Punch down the dough and shape it into small buns.
Place the buns on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch of space between each bun.
Bake the buns for 20 minutes, or until they are golden brown.
While the buns are baking, heat the plant-based milk in a small saucepan over low heat.
Once the buns are done, brush them with the warm milk and serve immediately.
